AUTHOR: DOCK-OPS-2
COURIER-LIEUT-NULL
VESHRIN-FOREMAN-3
BUREAU-DESK-7
BRANWEN-SHIFT-4
HEADLINE: Switchyard rail seven timing irregular for sixth shift running.
Three handles failed to file return-routes overnight.
Maintenance section 17-D sealed for the second time this week.
Ore haulers reporting longer pre-dawn cycles than scheduled.
Boiler ward reports red-chalk warnings refreshed by an unknown hand.
Underpass service light stuck on red. Chalk crossings unauthorised.
BODY: Halen-Coil atmospherics flagged minor pressure drift in adjacent decks. No public alert. Couriers Guild advises caution.
Bureau watch is aware. Hydroponics holding the line on rations until further notice.
House Branwen welders refused the call-out. No public reason given.
Veshrin Industries surface-mining detail returned with two haulers fewer than departed. No comment from the Foreman.
Recovery Bureau acknowledges receipt. Recommends 'continued passive observation'. Hydroponics declines to comment.
Patch this once, file it twice. Repair crews have been instructed to leave the second filing blank.

alley_notice.title: Notice Nailed to the Payphone

NOTICE: REWARD for the return of a Branwen-issue toolkit lost near the boiler ward last shift. Tools are sentimental, not valuable. Bring them to Mikra; she'll know.
If you see Halver Ngo, tell him the cart's open and the broth is still warm. Don't tell him I asked.
The clinic has revised its synthblood ration schedule. Effective immediately. Patients with active chrome should consult.
Anyone with a confirmed sighting of the rust-mite cloud near the underpass: come find me. I pay in dock tokens.
Bureau handles are no longer welcome at this cart. The broth pot is for people who pay in something other than questions.

overheard_fragment.title: Overheard at the Cart

WHEN: during the Bureau patrol shift change
OVERHEARD: Three runners short tonight. Either the Bureau took them or they're hiding from somebody worse.
Half's still asking about the envelope. Pretend you didn't hear me. Tell him the cart's closed if he comes by.
Tamsin's been pulling shelves out of the deeper archive for two weeks. Don't ask why; you don't want to be on her list.
Mara's pricing routes again. Bad sign. Last time she did that, half of us couldn't go home for a week.
I heard Static actually got an answer. After the seven. He won't say what. He won't say WHO.
Vey came down off the relay stair without her tablet. I've never seen her without that tablet.
SPEAKER: a courier in faded Veshrin grey
a clinic orderly with iron-stained sleeves
a Branwen welder pretending not to be on shift
a House Strick lieutenant who shouldn't have been there
a scrubber on her break, not making eye contact with anyone

bureau_memo.title: Bureau Internal Memo

SUBJECT: Pattern of unauthorised maintenance access — Section 17-D
Surveillance of unregistered courier traffic — Switchyard rail 7
Monitoring of clinic stock movement — Backroom Clinic
Inventory discrepancy — Veshrin surface-mining detail
Handle activity flagged for review — Choir Channel correlations
BODY: Subject of interest: handle 'COURIER-LIEUT-NULL'. Recommend continued passive observation. Direct intervention not authorised at this time.
Three returns of interest. No formal action. The Bureau prefers patterns to interventions.
Halen-Coil filtration logs flag a discrepancy. Source unconfirmed. Continue routine monitoring.
Discrepancy of two handles between manifest and return roster. Veshrin foreman declined to comment. Memo retained for pattern analysis.
Three booth-bleed incidents flagged. Not one matches a registered audio source. The Recovery Bureau notes that this is, technically, a non-event.

archive_marginalia.title: Archive Marginalia

WHERE: a manifest sleeve dated three years before the Crisis
a binding in the buried vault no one was supposed to read
HAND: an older hand that pre-dates the Coil family
a hand that signs nothing — but the ink-pressure pattern matches a known Curator shift
NOTE: 'cross-reference KIROV-T-CLEAR with Halen-Coil priority manifests pre-Crisis. The buyer should not exist.'
'do not repair this. it is meant to be unreadable. the unreadable is the record.'
'Vey came in. We did not speak. She left the file open at the page I would not have shown her.'
'the choir channel runs through here too. I will not say where. I will not.'
